shared_data.description: Plant them to grow an oak tree.
<color=yellow>Valuable</color>
<color=yellow>Valuable</color>
An acidic smell still lingers. Prominently.
This sturdy leather is thick, and still warm to the touch.
A neck in its final stage of life.
The pelvic bone of a four legged creature.
These ribs have already been stripped clean of any meat.
A thick skull, with room for a surprisingly large brain.
Smells a bit smokey, even when raw.
The metal glints oddly in the sunlight. Somehow, you know that it can never be complete on its own...
What battles has this weapon borne witness to? You cannot see its past, only shape its future...
A bundle of barley.
Needs to be fermented.
Plant them to grow a beech tree.
This ancient fragment appears to be a piece of a broken bell...
Caustic bile drawn from the corpse of a gjall.
Plant them to grow a birch tree.
A thick and furry hide, coarse yet incredibly soft.
This fatty meat was once meant to sustain a body through hibernation.
Sharp claws suitable for picking berries – or for dismembering.
Filled to the brim with ancient power.
A block of solid stone, seamed with shifting colors.
A heavy bar of dark metal.
A twisted hunk of dark metal.
Wood hardened by fire and ash.
Thick enough to contain something volatile, yet fragile enough to be shattered.
The contents of a leech. Ick!
Best used with a catapult. Handle with care.
A pile of shattered bones.
A tasty, white fish meat. Very good for your bones!
It looks just like bone. Best not to question the anatomy of this creature too much...
This has caused the death of many a brave sailor.
Ready for the oven.
A strong alloy of copper and tin.
Used in construction of ships and furniture.
It's old and oxidized but can be smelted and used again.
When you crack open their shells, the meat within is tender and succulent.
Steep these in something flammable for a long lasting and cosy light source.
A plate of chitinous armour.
Plant these if you like carrots...
Best used with a catapult. Make sure nothing fragile is in the way.
The only remnant of the fallen valkyrie's former self.
No matter how hot this gets, the other side of it remains strangely cool.
A link of iron chain.
The resin from a tree that was set ablaze a long time ago. It's still ready to burn some more.
Followed by the distinct smell of burnt meat.
This could be used for some clever machinery...
The blackened skull of a long-dead warrior. It's unlikely that a proper burial would grant them any peace.
All chickens bear the ancestral curse of being delicious.
A shard of crustacean shell.
A lump of coal.
<color=yellow>Valuable</color>
A bar of pure copper ready to be worked.
Unrefined copper. Needs to be refined in a smelter.
One person's scrap is another person's treasure.
A shard of crystal from deep within the earth.
Elastic and strong. This tendon must have come from a quick and agile animal.
Some call it a weed, but it's pretty nonetheless.
A cleaned hide from a deer.
A fragment of a Dvergr sealbreaker.
Looks like a perfect piece for piercing something...
Parts of an old blade. If all the pieces were reassembled it could likely be made whole.
The hilt of a long forgotten sword. Perhaps it could be reforged if one had all the pieces...
This shard of metal looks like the tip of a sword. One might be able to reforge the blade if more pieces are found...
A restless essence of a once living thing...
This is the stuff of life, the poison that consumes itself. The Dvergr refine it to use in their esoteric designs.
An ancient and sturdy material.
A slimy length of something's insides.
The mystical power will be revealed another day.

It's hard to tell whether the steam coming off of this dish is because it's freshly cooked or because of the asksvin meat in it. Either way, the spiced meat together with the vineberries and fiddlehead sprouts is so delicious that it doesn't matter!

You won't be able to resist this platter of delights from the Black Forest! Venison sirloin steaks are served together with spiced thistles and carrots, and the whole meal is tied together with delicious jams. Just have one more bite!
A boar that has been roasted to perfection, glazed and served atop a bed of greens, with additional cuts of meat on the side. A feast like this is sure to fill your stomach and brighten your day!
The time has come for mushroom enthusiasts to rejoice! Try different kinds of mushrooms, mushroom marinated seeker meat and mushroom-filled misthare, and don't miss out on the tasty sap dressing.

Gather around this steaming pot full of deliciousness and warm yourselves up again after a day out in the cold. The usually tough and chewey wolf meat has become tender enough to practically melt in your mouth, and the onions and carrots are made even tastier with exciting spices.
Fish, fish, and more fish! And also serpent meat, cut to look like fish! Explore the flavours of the Ocean, along with some grilled greens for those still practicing their sea legs.
There's nothing plain about this feast! Enjoy pies and loaves fresh from the oven, both sweet and savoury, and experience the culinary equivalent of a hug.

Who knew that leeches were edible? With the correct preparation (lots of cooking and lots of seasoning) you will be able to serve them in this feast for the culinary brave. For those who have a sensitive stomach, there are also tasty skewers of sausages and turnips on the side.
A small pile of feathers.
High quality wood for fine carpentry.
Plant it to grow a fir tree.
This rocket's blasting off again!
This rocket's blasting off again!
This rocket's blasting off again!
This rocket's blasting off again!
This rocket's blasting off again!
This rocket's blasting off again!
This rocket's blasting off again!
Ready for the oven.
A good catch.
A good catch.
A withered metal from ancient times. No one knows what it was once used for.
According to legend, this metal was used by the gods themselves to craft powerful weapons.
A withered metal from ancient times. No one knows what it was once used for.
Warm to the touch with glowing veins of strange metal. Needs to be refined in a blast furnace.
Unspun fibers from a flax plant.
Can be shaped into sharp blades.
These plants carry a strong but pleasant scent. However, it's possible that not all creatures agree...
This mysterious organ keeps a perfect temperature.
The saltwater scent of this plant makes you think of the wide open ocean.
Light is reflected sharply off of this gem, or does it come from within the stone itself?
This gem pulses with energy, almost as if it were a living thing.
You wonder how many deals have been made to appease this gem. How many palms have bled onto it in exchange for its power?
Be careful not to puncture this while you carry it...
Porous yet sturdy.
The milky eyeball of a Greydwarf.
It smells like fermented fish.
A piece of very hard antlers.
The meat of a hare is scant but toothsome.
Ready for the oven.
A bar of pure iron ready to be worked.
Needed for advanced construction projects.
Unrefined iron. Needs to be refined in a smelter.
An empty vessel waiting to be filled with firewood and kindling.
It's old and rusty but can be smelted and used again.
Made from natural fibers and dvergr hair.
A sturdy, rough fabric.
[item_larva_description]
A small pile of leather scraps.
A fine linen thread made out of a strong flax filament.
A raw slab of marbled meat.
A heavy pelt of thick, musty fur.
Ready for the oven.
Ready for the oven.
The hand of man could hardly design a more perfect weapon.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Needs to be fermented.
Ready for the oven.
A mysterious contraption built by the Dvergr. Used to build traps.
Ready for the oven.
Potent energy swirls within, ready to be unleashed.
It's hard to believe it has ever beaten.
Chewy.
Inedible when raw, but proves to be quite a tasty snack if cooked.
The pointy end of a Deathsquito.
Dark volcanic glass.
Plant to grow a healthy onion.
Rotten and putrid-smelling. Why do you want this?
Plant it to grow a pine tree.
Ready for the oven.
A fragment of something brittle.
A fragment of something brittle.
Dragon egg is a hard and difficult material to work with, yet here it has been ground to a fine, glittering dust...
This unstable powder packs great potential.
An earthy odour clings to these dried lumps. It's best to not think too hard about what they are.
The queen of the bees!
Her majesty's will was hard and unrelenting, but this piece of carapace is perhaps even more so.
Sticky tree resin which insulates well. If put to the flame it burns slow and steady.
Ready for the oven.
An old root from an ancient tree stump. It feels both flexible and durable at the same time.
Perfect for building log cabins.
<color=yellow>Valuable</color>
Sacred blood from the Great Tree.
A pelt of glittering scales.
A slice of sea serpent. Smells fishy.
The shiny metal-like scale from a sea serpent.
A whetstone wheel ready to spin.
A protective force within is ready to be unleashed.
A bar of pure silver ready to be worked.
<color=yellow>Valuable</color>
Unrefined silver. Needs to be refined in a smelter.
It still fizzes softly with ancient memories.
Whatever spices have been used in this blend, they must come from someplace hot. And as if that wasn't enough, they have also been dried and smoked before being ground into a fine powder.
Thyme and marjoram carefully harvested and dried, before being mixed together. This blend will turn even the most basic of ingredients into a delicious feast.

A good herbalist knows exactly where to go to find ramsons, but she won't tell you the secrets of her trade! Still, if you have the coin for it you may treat yourself to this blend of finely harvested ramson bulbs and leaves.
Only the most seasoned herbalist can find the pepperwood tree and harvest its leaves and bark. After careful preparation it's ready to be added to food, and is sure to give it some warming heat.

The sour tang of sorrel, mixed with something the herbalist would rather not disclose, is well suitable for fish. Any fisherfolk worth their salt would do well to keep this blend near, in case of a good catch!
This blend utilises all aspects of the lovage plant, a rarity in the tenth world. Roots, leaves and seeds have all been gathered, and then mixed together in the perfect quantity.
It's a rock.
It's a stone.
Smells like rotten eggs.
It throbs with inner heat.
A sticky lump of tar.
Beautiful but prickly.
It is crackling with energy.
A bar of pure tin ready to be worked.
Unrefined tin. Needs to be refined in a smelter.
A thick and sturdy hide. This is why trolls are so hard to kill.
A dense vegetable, rich with nutrition.
Plant to grow a healthy turnip.
A relic of savage strength, perfect for forging armour that bears the wild’s fury.
Placeholder item for Yagluth item drop.
Ready for the oven.
Looks like they could crumble at any moment.
These unassuming seeds can grow into vines that might overtake entire buildings.
Given that this bird feasts on all kinds of things, you'd better cook its meat before you even think about eating it.
It keeps whispering jibberish...
A giant bone, knotted like old wood.
It is hard and sharp like iron.
Still sharp.
A bundle of thick, rough hair. It has a strong smell.
A pelt of shaggy fur.
Good, strong wood to build with.
Godflesh, wood from the Great Tree.
The earthy remains of the giant Ymir.
Fortifies you against fire.
A perfect balance of sweetness and acidity.
It's bloody tasty.
Tiny but tasty.
Lean and salty.
A tasty loaf of bread.
An orange treat.
A warm tasty soup made of mostly carrots.
The gold of the forest.
This meat has a potent and mature flavour, but is very tasty when grilled right.
Tastes like victory.
The boiling sea did nothing to this meat, but grilling it over the fire has given it a delightful crisp.
Succulent white meat. A true delicacy.
It tastes like chicken.
All that running paid off.
Sunny side up!
Stringy but flavorful.
A great hunk of tender meat, food fit for Valhalla!
An earthly taste.
A chewy and somewhat dry meat. Some seasoning would probably make it taste better, but it'll fill your stomach regardless.
A wild taste.
Fall-apart tender.
Crispy cool and creamy.
Veggies with a twist!
This musty stew is a necessity on every adventurer's menu.
Bounty from both land and sea.
A tasty side of smoked fish.
Bread and fish, what more to wish?
[item_healthupgrade_description]
[item_healthupgrade_description]
Sweet and tasty.
Grilled to perfection. Makes both eyes and mouths water.
Break the crust to release a cloud of fragrant steam. Delicious!
Bursting with magical flavour.
It's spicy, it's chewy, it's sweet… This mad dish tickles your tongue as well as your mind.
Leftover meat can actually be pretty tasty if you just mash it right!
The perfect drink for a day in the Plains.
Something poisonous stirs within, brewed to the point where its potential can finally be harnessed. But be careful to let the beast out...
Increases eitr regeneration.
Restores eitr.
Protects against the cold.
The squirrel must be quick on its feet as it runs up and down the trunk of the world tree. Although it no longer visits Valheim, there are still those who remember it...
Increases health regeneration.
Restores health.
Restores health.
Restores health.
This bottle is full, yet it feels like it weighs almost nothing at all.
Fortifies you against poison.
Increases stamina regeneration.
Restores stamina.
Restores stamina.
What creature can carry more than a troll? Why, a viking with this drink of course!
You feel invigorated just by holding this, as the ocean beckons you to come for a swim.
It smells like a pigsty. Likely the taste won't be much better.
The nectar of the Gods, divine mead.
An intense musk permeates the air around this bottle.
Battle fuel.
Chunks of goodness in a thick gravy.
One of life's Great Pleasures.
Bounty of the forest.
Glows with a soft blue hue.
Some say you can eat everything you find in the forest. That is not the case with this mushroom.
Swollen with magic, these golden orbs bubble from the ground where the Jotun fell.
The delicate blue magecaps crackle like nerve-endings with sorcerous vitality.
A delicious omelette with an earthy aftertaste.
Hopefully it tastes better after cooking.
An energetic glowing mushroom.
This savoury, charcoal-grilled meat has a slight aroma of seaweed and grass.
A crunchy and spicy taste.
Deliciously rich.
It takes some time and effort to make this pie, but the taste is well worth it.
Allows the consumer to quickly evacuate any misplaced meal and start anew.
That classic tasty blend of raspberries and blueberries.
Sweet and delicious.
This dessert keeps you going all day long.
There are maggots crawling in the meat. It smells awful.
Jelly fit for kings and queens.
Fresh, crisp leaves.
Links of savory, smoked meat.
A varied diet is important, so why not try this vegetarian option?
A quivering jelly with a taste like gentle electricity.
A cooked slice of sea serpent. Smells good.
Smells of honey and serpent...
Wakes you up!
This soup settles in your stomach with an almost tingly sensation.
Perhaps it's not the best flavour to start the day with, but it will give you the boost you need.
Sugary honey perfectly balanced with tangy fronds and tart berries.
[item_staminaupgrade_description]
[item_staminaupgrade_description]
[item_staminaupgrade_description]
Nutritious and restorative.
A sweet and tasty treat, to celebrate a feat!
These juicy berries are both sour and sweet.
Chewy and full of flavor.
Dripping with taste.
Made with sap from the great tree. Even a mouthful imparts a warm glow to your whole body.
A perfectly-balanced axe forged from dark metal with an emerald sheen.
A bright and burnished blade, curved like a smile.
Sharper than stone.
Sharp and strong, a woodcutter's friend.
Not even the giants of old could weather the poisonous bite of this weapon.
A crude axe for tree-felling.
If this axe could chop wood, wouldn't that be strange?
Handle with care.
If you hold it for too long your hand might just freeze in place.
The vial is hot in your hand, promising fiery destruction.
Should this vial break, caution is strongly advised.
Breathing in the fumes is not recommended.
The sticky substance within pulses, as if threatening you.
With enough heat, it solidifies upon explosion.
The stench is unbearable...
Everyone knows you can't breathe in the smoke.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A slow but powerful weapon.
A slow but powerful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
[item_spearflint_description]
Blood-drinker. A thirsty friend.
It brings light and warmth, drives back the darkness.
A darkling blade. Strong and sharp.
A butcher's knife designed specifically for slaughtering tamed animals.
A knife from the deep.
A glittering copper knife.
Sharpened flint. A reliable tool.
A savage piece of pain.
Some people would say this knife is better for butter.
A headache on a stick.
Dense yet spiked flametal, perfect for bashing enemy faces in.
The blood all but soaks into this mace, and it always yearns for more.
Particularly effective on cloudy mornings.
If the force of your blow isn't enough to knock your enemies to the ground, perhaps the primal roots will hold them down for you.
A fist-sized lump of iron on a wooden shaft.
A deadly weapon, bristling with fiendish spikes.
The dead fear silver. Remind them why.
One has to wonder why someone made this instead of a simple club.
A sturdy spear with a head of burnished bronze.
Sharpened to jagged perfection, this spear is sure to be deadly.
The ocean's wrath.
Despite its gnarled look, this spear is strong and perfectly balanced.
If your eye marks a thing for death, let your arm send the messenger.
Split your enemies' hearts in two.
A small sacrifice must be made for every battle...
Let the crack of thunder split the air.
Nature's forces burst through the ground wherever this spear strikes.
Even in death, the wolf's tooth aches for flesh.
This is just as sharp as a real spear, if a real spear was made out of wood.
A thing of death and beauty. It catches the light with a greenish glow.
Blood-drinker. A thirsty friend.
Cheater!
The sword that was broken is whole once more. Its flames burn hot, fuelled by the memories of ancient warriors.
The straight line between life and death runs along the edge of this blade.
Unsheathed, it sizzles and spits with deathless fire, an impossible blade.
The faint glow seems to slice through the mist.
Named after the evil dragon that dwells by the roots of the world tree, this sword heralds doom for those who cross its path.
If you bleed, your foes are sure to do so as well.
The power of lightning dances along the blade of this sword, a promise of the pain to come.
Tangle your foes in roots, like the namesake of this blade.
Purest of metals, nothing unclean can abide its touch.
Mostly harmless, unless you're very persistent.
Skål!
It can hold a lot of mead!
One year since we arrived... Skål!
Odin's finest warriors deserve the finest drinks.
A crude but functional bow.
Risen again from the ashes, this bow holds unyielding strength.
Arrows loosed from this bow will tear into flesh with unmatched ferocity.
Like the twisting branch was made to seek the sun, this bow was made to seek the slaughter.
Let your arrows fly as swift as the lightning strikes.
Dark wood strung with glistening sinew. A vicious thing.
A simple bow of strong and supple wood.
Finely worked and strung. A huntsman's joy.
Using this bow is backbreaking work but so worth it.
A simple bow.
A simple bow.
A simple bow.
A simple bow.
A simple bow.
A slow but powerful weapon.
Rips your foes apart, simple as that.
Ready to rend your enemies to pieces.
The bolts will tear through your enemies like a particularly nasty gale.
If the bolt doesn't pin your foe in place, the roots surging up from the ground surely will.
A simple bow.
A simple bow.
A simple bow.
Banded with hoops of iron, a true warrior's companion.
Fashioned from the strongest metal, able to turn even the deadliest blades.
A tower shield of gleaming dark metal.
The bones of dead warriors make for a good protection.
A shield of burnished bronze, good to turn a blade or two.
The almost unbreakable carapace of your enemies makes an excellent shield.
The skull of a seeker is solid but not heavy, which makes it perfect for a small and agile shield.
The shield is and always will be a viking's most important weapon.
The best defence is a great defence.
Its lightness and curved center makes it excellent for deflecting attacks.
An iron sword-breaker, tile of the battle-wall.
A tall shield of strong iron.
A wooden shield reinforced with iron. UNUSED
A sturdy shield of overlapping scales.
A shield of radiant silver.
A simple wooden shield.
A rough but heavy wooden shield.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
A crude but useful weapon.
A crude but useful weapon.
As the first man of Midgard, Ask knew it was important to not draw too much attention to himself.
Let the primal rage of a mighty bear consume you.
Inspiring fear in foes and courage in kin when worn.
This will help to keep your brains inside your skull.
People might say you look like a giant ant. But they will only say it once.
You simply can't help but feel happy when donning this cap!
An elaborate and finely-crafted helm.
A portable perpetual lightsource for the dungeon explorer.
The eyes of the beast were wise and knowing, so that it could measure the strength of a warrior in one glance.
This catchy hat may only be reeled in by the most seasoned adventurers.
While you're wearing this helmet, your enemies will think twice before trying to bite your head off.
A blue practical headscarf.
A simple yet fashionable purple cap.
A fancy green headscarf.
A warm fur cap, made from the finest leather.
A warm cap for special occasions.
A simple yet fashionable red cap.
A practical yellow headscarf.
A fancy red headscarf.
A warm fur cap, made from the finest wool.
A warm cap for special occasions.
A helm of polished iron, fit for a hero.
A hood of toughened leather.
Sorcery shows itself in the eyes, so most mages wear cowls to disguise their occult pursuits.
Even the first sorceress valued the mystique of covering one's face. It is rumoured that honouring her this way will make your spells even more powerful.
Celebrate summer with a crown woven from flowers.
Odin's finest warriors deserve the finest cloth.
A snug fit, finely made.
This hat is sure to add a bit of magical flair to any outfit.
Your head fits perfectly inside this knot of roots and bark.
The perfect way to avoid sunstroke.
It feels a bit...moist.
Trollskin is hard to work but makes exceptional armour.
A red cap in the style of house gnomes.
The first man in Midgard knew how to guard his most vital organs, perhaps with a breastplate just like this one.
If these intricate designs can protect warriors from distant lands, they can surely protect you too.
Crafted from the shattered bones of a tormented bear.
A breastplate of hammered bronze.
A breastplate crafted from the burnished carapace of a giant insect.
A plain brown dress, worn over a blue underdress.
A simple dress for everyday wear.
A brown dress, with a matching shawl around the shoulders. Warm and stylish!
A fancy brown dress, with beads and silver fibula brooches. Excellent for a feast!
A plain blue dress, worn over a red underdress.
A blue dress, with a green shawl around the shoulders. Warm and stylish!
A fancy blue dress, with beads and bronze fibula brooches. Excellent for a feast!
A plain yellow dress, worn over a green underdress.
A yellow dress, with a purple shawl around the shoulders. Warm and stylish!
A fancy yellow dress, with beads and silver fibula brooches. Excellent for a feast!
The beast could draw deep breaths, so that its howl could be heard far across the land.
This fusion of mysterious flametal and charred bones serves as a protective layer more resilient than anything you could ever find in Midgard.
When working the fields, it's important to dress accordingly. A shorter tunic lets in a cool breeze.
When working the fields, it's important to dress accordingly. This long dress keeps your knees covered while you work.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
A tunic made from animal hide.
These artfully layered robes have spells and charms sewn into every seam and fold.
Imbued with the power of the first sorceress, these robes will grant a boon to any who seeks to wield the ancient art of magic.
Finely wrought and strong enough to turn even the sharpest blades.
Better than nothing.
Finely wrought and strong enough to turn even the sharpest blades.
Trolls are hard to skin but their leather is tough and warm.
A plain blue tunic, brightly dyed.
A simple tunic for everyday wear.
A blue tunic, worn with a fashionable cape made from fine brown wool.
A blue tunic worn with beads and silver jewelry. Sure to attract attention from other vikings.
A plain red tunic, brightly dyed.
A red tunic, worn with a fashionable cape made from fine green wool.
A red tunic worn with beads and bronze jewelry. Sure to attract attention from other vikings.
A plain yellow tunic, brightly dyed.
A yellow tunic, worn with a fashionable cape made from fine purple wool.
A yellow tunic worn with beads and silver jewelry. Sure to attract attention from other vikings.
A wolfskin jerkin, warm and wild-looking. It protects against the cold.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
Sharper than flint. A sleek messenger of death.
Heavy and pointy, this one's gonna hurt.
This arrow has been whittled into shape from a charred femur, and it's as hard as any metal.
This arrow burns whatever it pierces.
A hide-breaker with a head of flint.
A shard of piercing ice.
Capped with iron and flighted with dark feathers.
The final stitch.
A sliver of darkness.
A bitter sting from afar.
A needle to calm restless spirits.
An arrow of sharpened wood.
A sleek bolt of dark metal.
A crude bolt of yellowed bone.
A heavy and solid bolt.
A sturdy bone from a forearm, shaped into a deadly bolt.
A sturdy iron missile.
Fire it with a bow.
Common dvergr fishing bait. Fishing rod sold separately.
Some fish already like it where the waters are warm, and this bait brings the temperature close to boiling.
This bait doesn't look like much, but it's a treat to fish that live where it's cold and dark.
It's not very nutritious, so the only fish that'll take this bait are the ones that are used to just eating ice.
Dead trolls in the forest often attract a fish or two, speed up the process with this bait!
A bait to guide the fish to you through shrouded waters.
This bait sinks deep, deep enough to lure the fish swimming along the very bottom of the sea.
You're not immune to deathsquito bites, and the fish here aren't immune to this snack!
It might smell foul, but the fish drawn to this bait are used to so much worse.
The first man to roam Midgard preferred to tread lightly. Wearers of these trousers could learn a thing or two from him.
It covers all the important bits, but not much else.
Thick hides and bone fragments woven into a rugged skirt.
Bronze greaves to shield your legs.
Leg guards of a rigid carapace.
The legs of the beast were lean and strong, so that it could leap great strides.
Heavy trousers insulate against the heat, while solid greaves keep your shins safe from low blows.
Iron greaves to protect your legs.
They squeak a little when you walk.
The trousers worn by mages are always especially tight. Discomfort fuels the focus that is needed for magic...
Whether or not the first sorceress actually wore trousers exactly like these, we can never know.
Expertly crafted leg protection.
A simple remedy for nudity.
A light armour oddly woven together by ancient roots and bark.
Leggings of tough troll hide.
Shaggy breeches of wolfskin.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
An iron scale mail, this will turn all but the strongest of blows.
It's been dead so long that it started living again.
